message:
fix(sync): prevent potential deadlock by waking pool wakers outside lock scope

Restructure waker notification in ResourcePool and PooledResource to
collect wakers while holding locks, then wake them after releasing.
Previously, waker.wake() was called while state/return_wakers mutexes
were still held, which risks deadlock if a waker's wake implementation
tries to re-acquire the same lock (e.g. re-entrant polling).

Changes in src/sync/pool.rs:
- PooledResource::notify_return_wakers(): drain wakers into a Vec
  before dropping the lock, then wake outside
- ResourcePool::process_returns(): accumulate waiters_to_wake across
  all return channel messages, drop the return_rx lock, then wake all
  collected wakers at once
- ResourcePool::close(): drain state.waiters into a Vec<Waker> before
  dropping the state lock, then wake outside
- Minor rustfmt reformatting of long method chains

Add test `pooled_resource_notifies_wakers_outside_return_waker_lock`
which uses a custom ReentrantReturnWaker that probes whether the
return_wakers mutex is free when the waker fires, verifying the
deadlock fix works correctly.

Changes in src/http/pool.rs - add edge-case coverage:
- `remove_nonexistent_is_noop`: removing from empty pool doesn't panic
- `release_nonexistent_is_noop`: releasing unknown connection is safe
- `mark_connected_nonexistent_is_noop`: marking unknown connection is safe
- `pool_default_config`: verify PoolConfig default values
- `acquire_prefers_idle_over_expired`: verify pool prefers fresh idle
  connections over expired ones during acquisition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
